violations.description: Wall-clock performance assertion using Stopwatch with a hard 2000ms threshold. This will flake under CI load, cold JIT, slow agents, or heavy parallel execution. The assertion measures HttpClient.GetAsync latency, which is unbounded in shared infrastructure.
violations.suggestion: Remove the timing assertion entirely (it is not a functional contract). If performance must be tracked, gather metrics via a separate perf-budget pipeline that runs on isolated hardware and tracks p95 over time, not a single-shot hard threshold in a smoke test.

violations.description: Wall-clock performance assertion using Stopwatch with a hard 3000ms threshold around Blazor Server hydration + shell render. Browser cold start + SignalR negotiation + hydration combined with cold JIT and Playwright Chromium launch make this assertion inherently non-deterministic across CI runs.
violations.suggestion: Drop the Stopwatch assertion. Rely on Playwright's WaitForSelectorAsync (already present) as the actual contract — the test still proves the shell renders. Track render perf separately if needed.

violations.description: ClickAsync immediately followed by page.Url.ShouldContain("/commands") without WaitForURLAsync. Blazor Server navigation is asynchronous (SignalR roundtrip + client-side router update); reading page.Url synchronously after the click may capture the pre-navigation URL and produce intermittent failures.
violations.suggestion: Insert await page.WaitForURLAsync("**/commands", new PageWaitForURLOptions { Timeout = 3000 }); before reading page.Url. Same pattern is used correctly in Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests at lines 99-101.

violations.description: Classic TOCTOU port allocation race: socket binds to ephemeral port 0, captures the assigned port, disposes the socket (releasing the port), and only afterward the WebApplication binds via Args=[$"--urls={BaseUrl}"]. Between socket dispose and Kestrel listen, another process or parallel test can grab the same port, causing intermittent EADDRINUSE / start-failures. The using-block scope ends before _app.StartAsync, widening the window.
violations.suggestion: Use the SO_REUSEADDR / no-bind approach: tell Kestrel to bind to port 0 directly via builder.WebHost.UseUrls("http://127.0.0.1:0") or KestrelServerOptions.ListenLocalhost(0), then read the resolved port from app.Urls or IServerAddressesFeature after StartAsync completes. This eliminates the release-rebind race entirely.

violations.description: Pure CSS-class selector 'a.skip-to-main' is used in a state-gating assertion (CountAsync().ShouldBeGreaterThan). The class name is purely presentational and can be renamed during refactor without any compiler warning, silently failing this accessibility check.
violations.suggestion: Anchor the selector on the semantic role/text instead: page.GetByRole(AriaRole.Link, new() { Name = "Skip to main content" }) or a data-testid (data-testid="skip-to-main").

violations.description: Pure CSS-class selector '.stat-card-grid' gates the dashboard-renders assertion. If the CSS module class is renamed or the grid is restructured, the test fails for the wrong reason (it should assert dashboard content rendered, not that one specific class name exists).
violations.suggestion: Use a data-testid (data-testid="dashboard-stat-cards") or assert via a semantic role/aria-label on the container.

violations.description: Selector '.admin-sidebar:visible' uses a pure CSS class plus jQuery-like :visible pseudo as the state anchor for the entire repeated-toggle test. If the sidebar root class is renamed, this test silently no-ops (a different selector evaluates to zero elements) instead of failing on the actual collapse/expand behaviour.
violations.suggestion: Anchor on the existing semantic nav landmark (page.Locator("nav[aria-label='Main navigation']").Locator("xpath=ancestor::*[contains(@class,'admin-sidebar')][1]")) or add a data-testid='admin-sidebar' to the host element.

violations.description: State gating via raw class-string parsing: bool actuallyCollapsed = sidebarClass.Contains("collapsed"). This is fragile — adding any other class containing the substring 'collapsed' (e.g., 'is-collapsed-mobile', 'pre-collapsed-anim') would yield a false positive. A pure-presentational class is also used as the source of truth for behaviour.
violations.suggestion: Expose collapsed state via aria-expanded on the toggle button or via data-collapsed='true|false' on the sidebar root, then assert that attribute directly. The CSS class can remain a presentational concern and refactor-free.

violations.description: WaitForShortcutRegistrationAsync probes 'window.hexalithAdmin?._shortcutHandlers?.size > 0' — the leading underscore signals a private/internal field of the JS module. Renaming/repackaging the field (a non-breaking refactor from the JS side's perspective) would cause this hydration wait to time out across every test that calls it.
violations.suggestion: Expose a public ready-promise or a public 'isShortcutSystemReady' getter on window.hexalithAdmin and await that instead of the private collection. Better: dispatch a 'hexalith:shortcuts-registered' DOM event and await it from Playwright.

recommendations: Remove both Stopwatch + hard-threshold assertions (SmokeTests.cs:75, BrowserSmokeTests.cs:87). Wall-clock perf budgets do not belong in functional/smoke E2E — they flake under CI load and cold JIT. If perf SLOs matter, track them in a dedicated benchmark pipeline.

Add WaitForURLAsync before page.Url assertion in BrowserSmokeTests.cs Navigation_CommandsPageLoads (line 69). The same correct pattern already exists in Dw5TypeCatalogNavigationBrowserAtddTests — apply it here.

Eliminate the TOCTOU port-allocation in PlaywrightFixture.cs by letting Kestrel bind to port 0 directly and reading the resolved port from IServerAddressesFeature after StartAsync — never use the bind/release/rebind pattern.

Replace presentational CSS-class selectors (.skip-to-main, .stat-card-grid, .admin-sidebar:visible) used in state-gating assertions with semantic roles, aria-labels, or data-testid attributes. Reserve CSS classes for styling only.
Stop parsing class strings as state ('Contains("collapsed")'). Expose collapsed state as aria-expanded or data-collapsed='true|false' and assert that machine-readable contract instead.
Replace coupling to window.hexalithAdmin._shortcutHandlers (private field) with a public ready-signal — either a 'hexalith:shortcuts-registered' DOM event or a public isReady getter.
summary: Four HIGH determinism violations (two wall-clock perf assertions, one click-then-URL race, one TOCTOU port allocation in the shared fixture) combined with four MEDIUM CSS-class-only state selectors put this suite well into the F band; the perf assertions and port race will flake intermittently in CI even when product code is unchanged.
